channel.title: Off The Rails: Federico Sapuppo
channel.description: Field notes from a Ruby on Rails developer. Bug autopsies, refactor diaries, and lessons from the Rails Way.

item.title: The legal assistant that couldn't be just one agent
item.description: A single assistant read a client's confidential file and answered Instagram DMs from the same memory. Holding private data, untrusted input, and a way out, it was one poisoned message from a leak. So I rebuilt it on Mastra as a team of role-scoped agents where no one holds all three legs of the lethal trifecta, plus a captcha trick, an upstream bug fix, and a 40-minute outage along the way.
item.author: fedesapuppo@gmail.com (Federico Sapuppo)

item.title: How I load anonymized production data into dev with evil_seed
item.description: A teammate's overnight database restore filled the disk before he could log in. I had been refusing the production dump for months and living on seeds. Then I was asked to ship a comprehensive anonymized demo DB. Top-down with evil_seed kept ballooning; bottom-up worked, and the dump dropped to a fraction of the original.
item.author: fedesapuppo@gmail.com (Federico Sapuppo)

item.title: How two AIs made me wipe my Mac
item.description: I asked Claude for the biggest folders, got a sudo rm -rf for 48 GB of iOS simulators, double-checked it with Gemini, and lost my entire home folder. What actually saved me was backups and a scripted machine rebuilder.
item.author: fedesapuppo@gmail.com (Federico Sapuppo)

item.title: Sergio rides shotgun
item.description: Filing a tax return without opening a browser, drafting a labor settlement from a voice memo at a toll booth, and turning a Hermes deploy into a product.
item.author: fedesapuppo@gmail.com (Federico Sapuppo)

item.title: From 3 AM deploys to a notifications skill: shipping web push with Noticed and AI help
item.description: Migrating 25 legacy mailers to email + PWA web push at scale, building a reusable skill, and chasing a nasty cross-user subscription bug.
item.author: fedesapuppo@gmail.com (Federico Sapuppo)

item.title: If your MacBook falls off a desk, install Omarchy
item.description: From a cracked M3 Pro to a batch of merged PRs in Omarchy 3.5, plus one kernel patch for the T2 SMC.
item.author: fedesapuppo@gmail.com (Federico Sapuppo)
